text: (G)I-DLE's message, combined with their personalities and the fact that they are heavily involved in their lyrics, music, and concepts, is why it works. It's a combination. People often clown their lyrics, but they can't deny that 1. they are more often than not catchy as hell, and 2. they communicate exactly what they need to communicate. My favorite example is Wife. People made fun of I cook cream soup, but we all know what they meant. It's funny and catchy as fuck, and the rest of the lyrics are camp and satire perfection. And if Korean fans are correct, and I can't verify it myself since I'm not Korean, it also uses a lot of clever wordplay.
label: Analytical Take

text: 100% she's always loved rapping so she was lyric writing even before IOI days. And she's incredibly savvy about toeing the line between annoying/repetitive and lyric heavy. Because if it's too repetitive people get bored, if it's too lyric heavy people also get bored.
label: Analytical Take

text: I haven't seen an idol being so honest in a long time, which makes me wonder if something is going on behind the scenes. For those who don't know, a few of the things she addressed during the livestream were weight loss, how people only talk about her looks, what she would change about the kpop industry, and how a lot of it is a facade and a scam. She also stated that the idol life is very far away from who she really is, that she hopes we won't be surprised when we get to see the real her, and that Giselle is what we make her out to be. I feel like these are kind of things you would never see an idol say. Especially in her position. I'm just wondering if she decided to be this open because of something changing about the contract. What do you guys think?
label: Analytical Take

text: I genuinely applaud her bravery so much. I was getting scared for her watching it but what she said has had to be said for a long time. I'm so glad she broke out of character for this. I'm just scared for the consequences coming to her from her company.
label: Reactive Take

text: Idols get dragged if they do and dragged if they don't. Kpop stans talk endlessly about idol's looks. Idols know this. Their looks are one dimension of what it takes to be successful in the kpop industry. Not exactly everything, but it is important. Fans will post photos of ugly idols and mock them disregarding their talent. So in this industry, it is paramount to an idol's career to look appealing. Some will feel like they need plastic surgery to do so. I don't think they should be judged for that.
label: Analytical Take

text: Plastic surgery is not as common in South Korea as the internet seems to think it is. Plastic surgery capital of the world is only in the sense that more foreigners travel to South Korea for work done than any other country. Don't believe everything without evidence, especially in regards to South Korea. There is so much blatant misinformation.
label: Analytical Take

text: It's pretty common in the West as well. Plenty of Americans get plastic surgery to varying degrees. I work in medical imaging and so many women get breast augmentation. Cosmetic work should always be a personal choice and never forced on anyone. Unfortunately we live in a society all over the world that values beauty, and men and women will do what it takes to fit in.
label: Analytical Take

text: This is the most random and intriguing piece of news I've come across in a while. If some of you aren't familiar, Tablo is a rapper from Epik High. Haru is his daughter, who was born in 2010. She previously participated in writing lyrics with her dad, but not in a million years would I have expected her to be credited as the sole lyricist of a title track for a group like RIIZE. Tablo is a genius lyricist in both English and Korean, so I'm really curious to see how his teenage daughter measures up.
label: Analytical Take

text: I have a 14 year old niece who is deeply influenced by kpop and kdrama. Yesterday I told her she looked like an ulzzang and she thought it was an offensive word. I was confused and amazed because I never expected ulzzang to be forgotten in this era. I was a kpop and kdrama fan since 2009 and I never felt this old.
label: Preference Take

text: Ulzzang was a thing from when I was in junior high in 2009. The general public doesn't have that sort of attention span on things.
label: Analytical Take

text: How would each SM group work as a family tree? This is just a fun little discourse. So far I have Girls' Generation as the mother of Hearts2Hearts and Red Velvet as Girls' Generation's weird sister with their even weirder daughter aespa.
